label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about the history and evolution of communication devices, specifically the telephone and mobile phones. The user wants to know what the main topic is. First, the passage starts with Alexander Graham Bell inventing the telephone in 1876. It mentions how the telephone was revolutionary but now we use it for more than just talking, like taking photos or internet access. Then it moves on to mobile phones, talking about the first mobile call in 1973 by Martin Cooper. It describes how mobile phones became popular, first as status symbols among "yuppies" and then became more widespread in the mid-90s with cheaper prices. It also discusses the impact of SMS on communication and how it changed timekeeping and language. The options are A) Alexander Graham's invention, B) SMS as a new way, C) New functions of mobile phones, D) Development of the mobile phone. Looking at the passage, it's not just about Bell's invention (A) because it goes on to talk a lot about mobile phones. B is mentioned as part of the changes, but SMS is only a part of the story. C mentions new functions, but the passage is more about the overall development over time rather than just the functions. D
